level_signals: 3–5 years of experience in Enablement, Program Management, Sales, or Customer Experience roles
California salary range $148,000–$170,000 consistent with mid-to-senior IC
Program owner and cross-functional collaborator, no direct reports mentioned

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3–5 years of experience in Enablement, Program Management, Sales, or Customer Experience roles
Understanding of key Sales roles, GTM processes, and measurement to effectively partner with Sales leadership on initiatives that strengthen core Sales capabilities and drive revenue growth.
Training delivery experience or an aptitude to deliver in-person/virtual training to Field teams in an engaging and enthusiastic manner
Strong project management skills, with a proven ability to prioritize and drive complex initiatives to completion
Comfort operating autonomously, with experience collaborating across remote and in-office environments
Effective communicator and facilitator, skilled at simplifying complex information for diverse GTM audiences
Experience with global stakeholder management, including Field leadership and cross-functional teams
Proficiency with enablement platforms, Salesforce, LMS systems, and content management tools

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in SaaS or Cloud Technology, ideally within the DXP space
Track record in enablement program design, including role-based learning, gamification, and performance tracking
High emotional intelligence, interpersonal fluency, and the ability to build trust across diverse teams
Growth mindset with a bias for action, resilience, and a touch of humor

explicitly_excluded: Visa sponsorship: 'this position is not eligible for visa sponsorship. Applicants must be authorized to work without the need for visa sponsorship by the start date of employment.'

scope.surface: Revenue Enablement programs across Sales, Solution Engineering, Customer Experience, and Partner Manager GTM teams
primary_metrics_inferred: KPIs and dashboards tracking enablement outcomes
Revenue growth tied to enablement programs
core_responsibilities: Design and deliver structured enablement programs tailored to Sales, Solution Engineering, Customer Experience and Partner Manager roles
Partner closely with our Sales and broader Field leadership team on initiatives that strengthen core Sales capabilities and drive revenue growth
Own cross-functional collaboration with Marketing, Finance, People and Operations to support company-wide enablement goals
Contribute to measurable performance by using KPIs, dashboards, and feedback loops to optimize enablement outcomes
Act as a program owner and facilitator, delivering in-person and virtual training, e-learning experiences, and onboarding integration
Ensure alignment with Sales Readiness tools, learning pathways, and content management best practices

green_flags: Comprehensive benefits package including 100% employee health premium coverage
Company paid parental leave and fertility benefits
Annual education budget and wellbeing stipend signal investment in employees
Clear scope: program owner with well-defined GTM stakeholder map
Global company with distributed workforce signals remote-friendly culture
red_flags: No visa sponsorship available — eliminates candidates needing authorization
Salary range stated only for California; compensation for other states not disclosed
Location type is ambiguous — JD mentions 'hybrid or distributed employees' in benefits but does not clearly state the work arrangement for this role
No explicit mention of seniority title (e.g., Senior vs. Manager level) despite comp suggesting senior IC

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years of experience requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from 'New hire office equipment stipend for hybrid or distributed employees' and in-person training responsibilities; not explicitly stated for this role)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'hybrid or distributed employees' benefit language and distributed workforce mention)
ambiguities: Location type: JD references both in-person training delivery and 'hybrid or distributed employees' in benefits section, but does not explicitly label this role as remote, hybrid, or onsite. Treated as hybrid.
Salary range is stated only for California applicants; compensation for other US states is not disclosed, creating uncertainty for non-California candidates.
Years range is stated as '3–5 years' which is narrower than typical; the upper bound of 5 years could signal this is not intended for very senior candidates.
